title: Velvety Indian Beef Curry
description: A buttery, velvety curry blending mixed beef cuts with coconut milk and aromatic spices, kosher-friendly and adventurous.

ingredients.name: Mixed beef cuts (chuck, sirloin, brisket)
ingredients.notes: Trim and cut into 2.5 cm cubes

instructions: Trim the beef and cut into 2.5 cm cubes.
Bring a pot of water to a boil, add the beef cubes, and blanch for 2 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking.
Heat coconut oil in a deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Stir in garlic paste and ginger paste, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add ground cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, and chili powder; cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in tomato puree and simmer for 5 minutes, letting the flavors meld.
Add the blanched beef cubes to the skillet and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Pour in the coconut milk, bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 15–20 minutes, or until the beef is tender and the sauce is velvety.
Season with salt to taste, and stir in a splash of lemon juice if desired.
Remove from heat, garnish with chopped cilantro, and serve hot over basmati rice or with warm naan.
Enjoy the buttery, velvety texture and aromatic adventure of this kosher-friendly beef curry.
